summary: Senior Full-Stack Engineer with 12+ years of experience shipping production products across Web3 security, B2B SaaS, marketplaces, and client-facing platforms. Built OpenZeppelin Defender 2.0 Secure Audit workflows with smart contract auditors and clients, helping plan, prioritize, and ship product work around findings, fix verification, and final reports. Strong fit for product engineering roles that need direct customer communication, React and Next.js ownership, Python-backed systems experience, and pragmatic use of AI tools such as Claude, Cursor, and the OpenAI API.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Co-led Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, working closely with smart contract auditors and clients to plan, prioritize, and ship TypeScript and React workflows for tracking findings, verifying fixes, and delivering final PDF reports.
Shaped audit-facing workflows around findings, remediation status, fix verification, and report delivery, making complex security work easier for auditors and client teams to follow.

Supported the Defender sunset by closing feature gaps, improving documentation, and working directly with enterprise users during migration to OSS Relayer, which covered most enterprise Defender users.

Owned the EVM side of the Rust-based OpenZeppelin Relayer OSS work, including transaction cost handling, RPC balancing, retry and failover logic, AWS KMS and GCP KMS signer integrations, and a fix for a critical nonce race condition.
Refactored the TypeScript monorepo build system, reducing builds from about 15 minutes to 3 minutes in local and CI runs.
Cut alert noise by about 60% across Slack and PagerDuty by tuning recoverable RPC and gas-spike alerts.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a client-facing MyPremise feature with React and TypeScript that surfaced up-to-date, often live data through one shareable URL instead of repeated PDF or PPT exports.
Built an AI report-generation proof of concept for natural-language reports, showing a faster way for teams to request and assemble data-driven reports.
Contributed to the REST to GraphQL migration with Apollo, improving API response times by 35% and lowering server usage.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Implemented real-time catalog and pricing updates with Go and Kafka, migrating core business logic from Python ETL so changes reached the app faster, contributing to a 21% CSAT increase and improved NPS.
Led an internal integration and onboarding platform, saving $75,000 annually and enabling faster onboarding for customer service teams.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Brought in as System Architect to revamp Vitamina's legacy technology stack and delivery process, then continued supporting the company in a consulting capacity with product, engineering, and infrastructure teams while cutting release cycles from months to about two weeks.

Modernized legacy product areas that were still tied to Internet Explorer by moving them from ASP.NET to Angular and from .NET Framework to .NET Core, enabling Chrome support and cleaner product iteration.
Introduced DevOps practices and led a staged move from on-prem systems to Azure hybrid infrastructure across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients, working end to end from discovery to implementation across medical, industrial, government, legal, and cultural sectors.

Worked directly with clients through discovery calls, demos, and status updates, including delivery of the PIIA project under a short deadline that helped open government opportunities for the agency.
Led architecture decisions for each project and set engineering standards for TypeScript, React, and React Native across the company.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Contributed to building Dxpress from idea to launch as one of the first Uber Eats-style delivery services in Chile, working with a small product and engineering team.
Focused on backend development across Python and Go services, building platform capabilities for search, geolocation, real-time operations, and product ETL workflows.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Contributed across B2B sales, medical, warehouse, ecommerce, and payment projects while growing frontend ownership and helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

company_profile.description: Sherlock is a Web3 security company that provides lifecycle security for onchain protocols before, during, and after launch.
company_profile.mission: Sherlock says its mission is to help secure an open, uncensorable financial system that billions of people can trust with real value.
company_profile.vision: Inferred from official materials: a security backbone for onchain systems that connects development, audit, and post-launch protection.
company_profile.industry: Web3 security and smart contract auditing
company_profile.business_model: Security products and services for Web3 protocols across development, audit, bounty, and coverage workflows.
recent_signals: Sherlock AI analyzes repositories and pull requests during development.
Official materials cite more than 2,000 critical findings and more than $250B in TVL secured.
Sherlock positions AI as a way to catch issues earlier while keeping human auditors in the loop.
